channel.title: +South on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +South on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Gajah Mada
item.description: (c. 1290 – c. 1364) of the Majapahit Empire, who rolled back Islamic states (Samudra Pasai) set up with Chinese navy backing in the malay peninsula, and united many parts of Indonesia - taking the vow - “Gajah Mada, the prime minister, said he will not taste any spice. Said Gajah Mada : If Nusantara (Nusantara= Nusa antara= external territories) are lost, I will not taste “palapa” (“fruits and or spices”).
